text: The patient is positioned in lithotomy with a slight Trendelenburg tilt. The surgeon reproduces the rectal prolapse by grasping the apex of the prolapsed tissue.

The surgeon resects the prolapsed rectum longitudinally at the three o'clock position using a reloadable 100mm GIA linear stapler. The stapler is held in place for at least one minute before firing due to the thickness and edema of the tissues.
The surgeon repeats the resection step at the nine o'clock position, resulting in the formation of anterior and posterior prolapsed rectal flaps.
The surgeon resects the anterior rectal flap horizontally at the anorectal junction, ensuring the resection is parallel to and avoids the dentate line.
The surgeon uses a reloadable endo-GIA linear stapling device to resect the posterior rectal flap, demonstrating the device's flexibility and ability to be angled during the procedure.

The surgeon uses a contour stapler for precise transection of the rectal prolapse. After the contour stapler is disengaged, the anal mucosa and neorectum spontaneously fall back into place, indicating the completion of the resection.
